questions.question: What is the primary tool you used in this course?
choices: A web browser extension that edits webpages
NotebookLM, a Google AI tool that works from sources you provide
A video editing software for creating summaries
An online database of pre-made video summaries
questions.feedback_correct: Exactly right — NotebookLM is a Google AI tool that synthesizes only from the sources you give it, with no outside web searches or hallucinated context.
questions.feedback_incorrect: NotebookLM is specifically a Google AI tool that works exclusively from sources you provide to it. It doesn't browse the web or pull in outside context — that's actually a key feature, not a limitation.

questions.question: Which of the following is NOT a content format you can generate in NotebookLM's Studio?
choices: Audio Overviews
Video Overviews
Live interactive webinars
Infographics
questions.feedback_correct: Correct — live interactive webinars are not mentioned as an available format in Studio.
questions.feedback_incorrect: Studio offers Audio Overviews, Video Overviews, Infographics, Quizzes, Reports, Flashcards and more. Live interactive webinars were not listed as an available format.

questions.question: What is the first step you take after opening NotebookLM and signing in?
choices: Add a website source immediately
Open the Studio panel
Create a new notebook with a meaningful name
Watch the 70-second interface video
questions.feedback_correct: Yes — creating a new notebook and naming it something meaningful is your first action after signing in.
questions.feedback_incorrect: After signing in, the first step is to create a new notebook by clicking the New Notebook button and giving it a name that means something to you. This creates your private workspace before you add sources.

questions.question: How do you know that NotebookLM has finished processing a website source you added?
choices: The browser tab automatically refreshes
You receive an email confirmation
The source appears in the Sources panel with a title and summary
A notification sound plays in the background
questions.feedback_correct: Perfect — when processing is complete, the source appears in the Sources panel on the left with a title pulled from the page and a short summary underneath.
questions.feedback_incorrect: When NotebookLM finishes processing your webpage, it will appear in the Sources panel (usually on the left) with a title and summary displayed. That's your confirmation that the material is ready to work with.

questions.question: What is the key difference between how NotebookLM generates content versus how a general web search engine might?
choices: NotebookLM is faster because it searches the entire internet at once
NotebookLM works only from sources you explicitly provide, with no outside context added
NotebookLM requires you to manually write prompts for every piece of content
NotebookLM can only process text and cannot handle images or videos
questions.feedback_correct: That's it — NotebookLM works exclusively from the sources you give it, which means the output is grounded and you can trace every insight back to your material.
questions.feedback_incorrect: The defining feature of NotebookLM is that it draws only from sources you provide. It does not browse the web or pull in outside context. This means everything it generates is traceable directly to your material, which is a strength, not a limitation.

questions.question: After you click Generate for a Video Overview, what should you expect to happen?
choices: The video appears instantly within one second
NotebookLM asks you to refine your request with more details
The process takes a moment — anywhere from a few seconds to a minute or two depending on the source
You are redirected to a separate website to download the video
questions.feedback_correct: Correct — generating a Video Overview is not instantaneous. It takes time because NotebookLM is building a structured presenter-style summary from your source material.
questions.feedback_incorrect: When you click Generate for a Video Overview, you need to be patient. The process can take a few seconds to a minute or two depending on your source material. This is normal — NotebookLM is doing the real work of synthesizing and structuring the content.

questions.question: What should you notice when you review the Video Overview that was created?
choices: The content includes information from multiple websites beyond your source
The structure and content draw directly from the source you added, not from anything else
The video includes ads and promotional content
The presenter adds their own opinions and interpretations not found in the source
questions.feedback_correct: Exactly — the Video Overview's topics, framing, and specific details all come directly from the source material you provided, which is what grounded AI output looks like.
questions.feedback_incorrect: The key observation when reviewing your Video Overview is that it stays tightly connected to your source material. The topics, framing, and specific details are all drawn from what you added — that's what makes it grounded AI output.

questions.question: Based on what you learned in this course, what would be the most practical next step to take this week?
choices: Sign up for multiple NotebookLM accounts to process different sources separately
Open a new notebook with something from your actual work and run the same steps
Wait until you take an advanced NotebookLM course before using it again
Share your first Video Overview on social media to get feedback
questions.feedback_correct: Yes — the recommended next step is to open a new notebook this week with actual material from your work and apply what you just learned.
questions.feedback_incorrect: The most practical next step is to apply what you learned with real material from your own work — a document, report, or webpage you've been meaning to read. That's where NotebookLM becomes genuinely useful to you.

questions.question: Why is the fact that NotebookLM does not perform open web searches considered a feature rather than a limitation?
choices: It makes the tool slower and more deliberate
It ensures the output is grounded in your material and every insight can be traced back to what you provided
It prevents you from learning new information
It means the tool only works with very short documents
questions.feedback_correct: Precisely — by working only from your sources, NotebookLM ensures grounded output that is traceable and reliable, with no hallucinated or outside context bleeding in.
questions.feedback_incorrect: NotebookLM's exclusive reliance on your sources is a feature because it makes the output grounded and trustworthy. You can verify every insight against your material, rather than wondering if the AI pulled in unverified information from elsewhere.

questions.question: What is the purpose of the written summary document that you can request at the end of the hands-on exercise?
choices: To submit to your supervisor as proof you completed the course
To keep as a reusable reference guide so you can repeat the steps on your own later
To share with other learners in the course
To test your memory of what you learned
questions.feedback_correct: Right — the written summary is a clean, step-by-step document you keep and reuse as a reference whenever you need to create a Video Overview again.
questions.feedback_incorrect: The purpose of the written summary document is to serve as your personal reference guide. You can keep it and use it anytime you want to repeat the steps on your own without having to remember every detail from the course.
